enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Match the words with their meanings.

Relie chaque mot anglais à sa signification en français.

Bloom's level: Remember (Se souvenir)

questions.texte: Match the words: cost, quality, quantity, price
questions.reponse: cost = coût, quality = qualité, quantity = quantité, price = prix

sousQuestions.texte: What word means 'the amount of money you pay for something'?
What word means 'how good something is'?
What word means 'how many or how much of something there is'?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Choose 'How much' or 'How many'.

Complète les questions avec 'How much' ou 'How many'.

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre)

questions.texte: Complete the questions with 'How much' or 'How many'.

sousQuestions.texte: ______ apples do you want?
______ rice do you need?
______ sugar is in the kitchen?
______ bottles of water do you have?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Complete the sentences with 'have got' or 'has got'.

Complète les phrases avec la forme correcte de 'have got'.

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer)

sousQuestions.texte: I ______ two brothers.
sousQuestions.reponse: have got (I have got two brothers.)

sousQuestions.texte: She ______ a new dress.
sousQuestions.reponse: has got (She has got a new dress.)

sousQuestions.texte: We ______ a big house.
sousQuestions.reponse: have got (We have got a big house.)

sousQuestions.texte: He ______ a red car.
sousQuestions.reponse: has got (He has got a red car.)

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Transform the sentences.

Transforme les phrases en négatives ou en interrogatives.

Bloom's level: Analyze (Analyser)

sousQuestions.texte: Affirmative: I have got a car. → Negative: ______
sousQuestions.reponse: I have not got a car. / I haven't got a car.

sousQuestions.texte: Affirmative: She has got a dog. → Interrogative: ______
Affirmative: They have got two cats. → Negative: ______
sousQuestions.reponse: They have not got two cats. / They haven't got two cats.

sousQuestions.texte: Affirmative: He has got a bike. → Interrogative: ______

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Ask and answer about prices.

Complète les dialogues entre un client et un vendeur.

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer)
images: A shop scene showing a customer asking a seller about the price of a bag, with price tags visible on various items

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the correct words.

sousQuestions.texte: Customer: '______ ______ does this bag cost?'
sousQuestions.reponse: How much (How much does this bag cost?)

sousQuestions.texte: Seller: 'It ______ 5000 francs.'
Customer: '______ you ______ a cheaper one?'
sousQuestions.reponse: Have / got (Have you got a cheaper one?)

sousQuestions.texte: Seller: 'Yes, I ______. This one is 3000 francs.'
sousQuestions.reponse: have (Yes, I have. This one is 3000 francs.)

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Ask about quantity.

Complète les questions avec 'How much' ou 'How many'.

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer)
images: A shopping scene showing various items: apples, rice, oranges, sugar, milk, bread on a supermarket shelf

sousQuestions.texte: ______ rice do you want?
______ apples are in the basket?
______ milk is in the fridge?
______ oranges do you need?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 7: Ask and answer about quality.

Utilise les mots donnés pour former des questions sur la qualité.

Bloom's level: Create (Créer)

questions.texte: Form questions about quality using the words in brackets.

sousQuestions.texte: (this / phone / good / quality) → ______?
(these / shoes / quality / good) → ______?
(that / bag / quality / bad) → ______?
(the / food / quality / good) → ______?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 8: Express likes and dislikes.

Utilise 'I like', 'I don't like', 'I love' ou 'I hate' pour compléter les phrases.

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer)
images: A person pointing at different items in a shop, showing happiness towards some and displeasure towards others

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct expression.

sousQuestions.texte: ______ this dress. It is beautiful! (like / love)
sousQuestions.reponse: I love (I love this dress. It is beautiful!)

sousQuestions.texte: ______ those shoes. They are ugly. (like / not like)
sousQuestions.reponse: I don't like (I don't like those shoes. They are ugly.)

sousQuestions.texte: ______ this bag. It is too expensive. (not want)
sousQuestions.reponse: I don't want (I don't want this bag. It is too expensive.)

sousQuestions.texte: ______ this cake. It is delicious! (like / love)
sousQuestions.reponse: I love (I love this cake. It is delicious!)

enonce.texte:
Exercise 9: Complete the shopping dialogue.

Complète le dialogue entre le client et le vendeur avec les mots donnés.

Mots à utiliser : cost, have got, like, don't want, how much, quality, quantity

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer) and Analyze (Analyser)
images: A customer and a seller in a shop, with the customer pointing at a product and the seller holding a notebook

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the words given.

sousQuestions.texte: Customer: '______ does this bag ______?'
Seller: 'It costs 10,000 francs.'
Customer: 'That is too expensive. ______ you ______ a cheaper one?'
Seller: 'Yes, I have. The ______ is good too.'
Customer: 'I ______ this one. I ______ that red one.'

enonce.texte:
Exercise 10: Write about your shopping preferences.

Écris un paragraphe de 4-5 phrases sur ce que tu aimes acheter et ce que tu n'aimes pas acheter.

Utilise les expressions suivantes :

  • I like / I don't like / I love / I hate
  • I have got / I haven't got
  • It costs / They cost
  • The quality is...

Bloom's level: Create (Créer)

questions.texte: Write 4-5 sentences about your shopping preferences.
questions.reponse: Example answer: I love shopping for clothes. I have got many dresses. They cost about 5000 francs each. The quality is good. I don't like buying shoes because they are expensive.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 11: Read the text and answer the questions.

Lis le texte sur le shopping et réponds aux questions.

Text:

"Marie loves shopping at the market. She has got 5000 francs in her bag. She wants to buy some fruits and vegetables. She likes apples and oranges. She doesn't like bananas. She asks the seller: 'How much are the apples?' The seller says: 'They are 500 francs per kilogram.' Marie says: 'I have got 500 francs. I want one kilogram of apples.' The seller also tells Marie that the oranges are good quality. Marie says: 'I love oranges! I will buy some.'"

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre) and Analyze (Analyser)
images: A woman at a market stall, pointing at apples and oranges, with a seller behind the counter

questions.texte: Answer the questions based on the text.

sousQuestions.texte: How much money has Marie got?
What does Marie like?
What does Marie not like?
How much are the apples per kilogram?
What does Marie say about the oranges?
sousQuestions.reponse: She says 'I love oranges! I will buy some.'

enonce.texte:
Exercise 12: Read and decide if the statements are true or false.

Lis chaque phrase et dis si elle est vraie (true) ou fausse (false).

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre)

questions.texte: Decide if the statements are true or false.

sousQuestions.texte: You ask 'How much is it?' for one item.
You ask 'How many is it?' for one item.
'How many apples do you have?' is correct.
'How much water do you need?' is correct.
'I have got a car' is the same as 'I have a car'.
